raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "The expansion of telehealth during the COVID-19 pandemic has transformed healthcare delivery.",
    "Telehealth has provided critical access to healthcare while mitigating COVID-19 exposure.",
    "Current telehealth flexibilities are contingent upon federal and state public health emergency declarations.",
    "Telehealth has ensured greater equity in healthcare access for communities of color and rural communities.",
    "Telehealth has become an essential tool for physicians, allowing them to provide safe and effective care.",
    "Telehealth connects patients to their care providers more quickly and efficiently.",
    "Many patients prefer to connect with their providers via telehealth.",
    "Telehealth has emerged as a critical pathway for patients to access time-sensitive sexual and reproductive healthcare.",
    "Barriers to accessing in-person health services, such as transportation and time away from work, existed prior to COVID-19.",
    "Telehealth has radically improved access to affordable healthcare, especially in rural communities.",
    "Telehealth allows safety-net providers to deliver critical health services through phone or video conferencing.",
    "AB 32 aims to make telehealth flexibilities permanent in California law."
  ]
}
```
